response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is primarily a full-stack web developer specializing in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with only a brief (~6 month) professional C++ engagement at National Instruments in 2014–2015. The role mandates 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, strong SQL Server or Oracle experience, and either fixed income domain knowledge or capital markets expertise paired with strong C++. None of these core requirements are meaningfully met: C++ is peripheral to his career, there is no evidence of Windows-platform C++ work, SQL Server and Oracle are absent, and while RIDE Capital provides minor financial services exposure, it is far from the fixed income or capital markets depth required.
strengths: Some C++ exposure through NI LabWindows component work and a C++-based bachelor thesis project
Brief financial services context via RIDE Capital investment platform role
Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps capability across multiple roles
concerns: C++ professional experience totals roughly 6 months versus the mandatory 5–10 years
No Windows-platform C++ development; career is predominantly Linux and web-stack oriented
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain experience

key_factors.factor: No capital markets or fixed income domain
